plan_adherence_notes: Relative to `ff/Tbayes-market-eu0-v2`, the cumulative branch diff follows the recorded plan closely. `backend/server.py` adds the read-only `preview_event_trade_position_net_change(...)` helper, keeps idempotent replay/conflict handling ahead of the new preview path, raises the planned `400 position_limit_exceeded` ApiError with the frozen message on strict over-cap results before accepted-command materialization, and refactors `sync_account_exposure_state(...)` to reuse the same projection while preserving zero-crossing cleanup and leaving `/risk` logic untouched. `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` adds the expected boundary, side-effect-free/idempotency, replay-bypass, helper, and surviving-position regression coverage. The only minor gap is that the written plan never explicitly called out the existing `market_not_active` precedence that remains ahead of the new preview gate.
missed_questions: Should the new max-position preview/`400 position_limit_exceeded` path take precedence over the existing `market_not_active` terminal rejection when both could apply to the same fresh submission, or should inactive markets continue to short-circuit first?
reason: Approve. The branch implements the planned EventTrade write-path patch, keeps over-limit submissions side-effect free and idempotency keys unbound, reuses the same net-position projection for accepted exposure sync, and does not change `/v1/accounts/{id}/risk` behavior. Validation ran `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_bayes_market_api.py'` and `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'`; both passed (219 tests and 260 tests).
